meaning: adj.心神不安的,不自在的,不稳定的,不舒服的adv.心神不安地,不自在地,不稳定地
examples: Mollie gave an uneasy little titter.
She felt uneasy in the unfamiliar surroundings.
He looked uneasy and refused to answer questions.
At dinner that evening I felt uneasy, as if I were concealing a secret.
But it has stirred up simmering resentments in the Alliance for Chile, the opposition's uneasy coalition.
